semantic_journey: Arabic malakha = to tear out; related to disturbance/madness in some contexts. Greek melankolia = melancholy (melas=black + khole=bile). Melankolia is a transparent Greek medical compound from humoral theory with no Arabic semantic input. No path from tearing-out to humoral melancholy.
key_insight: Greek melankolia is a transparent Greek humoral-theory compound; Arabic tearing-out has no path to it.

semantic_journey: Arabic qakha = insides rotted from disease. Greek kakektes = in bad habit of body, wasted (kakos=bad + hexis=bodily condition). Core: disease wasting the body from within. 1-step drift: internal disease-rotting -> externally visible wasted condition.
key_insight: Arabic internal disease-rotting and Greek bad-bodily-state/wasting share the core of disease destroying the body from within.

semantic_journey: Arabic khatala = to smear with liquid. Greek katakhutlon = something poured over / a pouring-vessel. Smearing and pouring are different liquid-application methods. No valid 2-step action-to-instrument path.
key_insight: Arabic smearing-with-liquid and Greek pouring-vessel describe different liquid-application methods with no valid path.

semantic_journey: Arabic kadasha = to inform partly, to drive animals. Greek skedastheis = having been scattered/dispersed. Partial informing and scattering are unrelated concepts. Driving animals does not mean scattering them.
key_insight: Partial informing or driving animals has no semantic path to scattered/dispersed in 2 steps.

semantic_journey: Arabic ghundar = a fat plump youth. Greek Gindaros = Gindarus, a place in northern Syria (proper name). A physical description of a fat youth does not plausibly name a fortified settlement. Proper name exclusion applies.
key_insight: Arabic description of a fat youth does not plausibly originate a Syrian place-name; proper name exclusion applies.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-asqafa = intended good but failed to do it (failed vigilance/incomplete effort). Greek alaoskopie = useless/blind watching (a-privative + laos + skope). Both encode deficient/failed vigilance: intention without execution (Arabic) vs watching without seeing (Greek). 1-step shared concept: deficient vigilance.
key_insight: Arabic failed-good-intention and Greek blind-useless-watching both encode deficient vigilance as a shared abstract concept.

semantic_journey: Arabic shakhama = milk souring/changing smell (quality change). Greek skhemassi = dative plural of skhema = forms, shapes, appearances. Milk souring and external forms/shapes share no semantic path.
key_insight: Milk souring (quality change) and forms/shapes (external appearance) are entirely different semantic domains.

semantic_journey: Arabic rabwa = breath in chest, panting, chest-swelling. Greek arabeo = to clatter, rattle. Panting/chest-swelling is respiratory; rattling/clattering is acoustic impact. The link requires 3+ steps. Greek arabeo appears onomatopoeic.
key_insight: Arabic chest-swelling/panting and Greek armor-rattling are unrelated; Greek arabeo appears onomatopoeic, not from Semitic chest-breath.
